sections.title: Market Pulse: Geopolitical Optimism Fuels Broad Rally

items.headline: Wall Street Rallies as U.S.-Iran Deal Progresses and SpaceX Debuts Strongly
items.summary: U.S. equities closed higher on Monday, driven by optimism surrounding a potential peace agreement between the United States and Iran, which sent oil prices tumbling. A landmark initial public offering (IPO) by SpaceX also contributed to a significant boost in investor confidence, leading to a broad 'risk-on' rally across major benchmarks. This positive sentiment overshadowed concerns about recent high Treasury yields and upcoming Federal Reserve decisions. Not financial advice.

items.headline: Space Exploration Technologies Corp. Soars on IPO Debut
items.summary: SpaceX ($SPCX) surged in its market debut, closing up 19.2% at $160.95 and pushing its market value to approximately $2.1 trillion, becoming the 6th-largest U.S.-listed company. This record-setting IPO capped immense demand for its leading position in space launches and satellite communications.

items.headline: Western Digital Rises on AI Optimism
items.summary: Western Digital ($WDC) saw significant gains, advancing 14.21% as investor appetite for artificial intelligence (AI) and semiconductor stocks increased amidst broader market optimism.

items.headline: AXT Gains in Early Trading
items.summary: AXT ($AXTI) climbed 14.8% in early trading, contributing to the day's positive momentum.

items.headline: Micron Technology Jumps on AI Enthusiasm
items.summary: Micron Technology ($MU) posted a strong performance, up 9.75%, benefiting from renewed AI optimism and a general shift towards growth stocks.

items.headline: Fox Corp. Tumbles on Acquisition Plans
items.summary: Fox Corp. ($FOX) experienced a significant decline, falling 17.8% in early trading, reportedly due to news concerning its Roku acquisition plans.

items.headline: Sasol Limited Drops Amidst Market Shifts
items.summary: Sasol Limited ($SSL) saw a sharp decline of 11.5% during the trading session.

items.headline: U.S.-Iran Peace Deal Leads to Oil Price Plunge and Market Rally
items.summary: A preliminary peace agreement between the U.S. and Iran to end hostilities and reopen the Strait of Hormuz significantly boosted global markets. Oil prices fell to a three-month low, with WTI crude trading around $80 per barrel, down approximately $4.64. This development reduced geopolitical risk and fueled a broad 'risk-on' rally, with investors shifting from energy to growth stocks.

items.headline: Federal Reserve FOMC Meeting Anticipated This Week
items.summary: The Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) meeting is scheduled for Tuesday and Wednesday this week, marking Chair Kevin Warsh's first meeting. Markets widely anticipate interest rates to remain steady at 3.50%-3.75%, with focus on the forward guidance and Warsh's inaugural press conference on Wednesday.

items.headline: Consumer Sentiment Improves in June
items.summary: The University of Michigan's consumer sentiment index for June was revised upwards to 48.9 from a previously reported 44.8, surpassing expectations of 46. This improvement was partly attributed to easing gasoline prices and was most pronounced among lower-income consumers.

items.headline: Sector Rotation Sees Tech and Financials Lead, Energy Lag
items.summary: On Monday, the Materials Select Sector SPDR (XLB) rose 1.8%, the Financials Select Sector SPDR (XLF) gained 1.4%, and the Utilities Select Sector SPDR (XLU) advanced 1.1%. Technology, artificial intelligence, semiconductor, and travel stocks also led the charge. Conversely, energy stocks lagged due to falling oil prices, and the Health Care Select Sector SPDR (XLV) saw a slight decline of 0.2%. Market leadership is showing signs of broadening beyond semiconductors, although AI spending continues to support the sector.

items.headline: U.S. Nonfarm Business Productivity Rises in Q1 2026
items.summary: Nonfarm business sector labor productivity increased by 0.3% in the first quarter of 2026. This modest gain reflected a 1.0% rise in output and a 0.7% increase in hours worked, marking the smallest productivity increase since Q1 2025.

items.headline: Goldman Sachs Reiterates 'Buy' on Nvidia
items.summary: Goldman Sachs analysts reiterated a 'Buy' rating on Nvidia ($NVDA), expressing confidence in the company's ability to sustain its growth profile into 2027 while maintaining a competitive edge in the market. Their CY27 estimates for Nvidia stand over 30% above street consensus.
